member.name: T:ReadyM.Relay.Client.ClientEcsUpdateLoop
member.summary: This class is responsible for scheduling ECS operations on the client side. The current implementation assumes that the update loop tick will be called externally from the game code, on the appropriate thread.

member.name: M:ReadyM.Relay.Client.IClientEcsUpdateLoop.Tick(System.Single)
member.summary: Executes one tick of the ECS update loop. Should be called regularly, e.g. from a game loop or a timer.
member.param: Time in seconds from the previous tick.

member.name: T:ReadyM.Relay.Client.Utilities.NetworkSessionStats
member.summary:
All-time network session stats: - Ping distribution via fixed histogram [0..MaxValidPingMs] (exact percentiles) - Packet loss (ping > MaxValidPingMs) + streak severity - Upload/Download rate in bytes/sec computed from LiteNetLib NetStatistics deltas (exact percentiles via stored samples)
Not thread-safe by design (we use a single network thread).

member.name: M:ReadyM.Relay.Client.Utilities.NetworkSessionStats.UpdateTransfer(LiteNetLib.NetStatistics,System.Double)
member.summary: Call periodically (ideally every ~1 second). Computes bytes/sec samples from LiteNetLib counters. nowSeconds must be monotonic (e.g., Time.realtimeSinceStartup in Unity).

members.member: Writes a structured log with all stats.

member.name: T:ReadyM.Relay.Client.Utilities.NetworkSessionStats.RateSeries
member.summary: Stores rate samples and computes exact percentiles by sorting a copy when dirty. For ~10k samples, this is cheap and simple.
